summary:
The agent emphasizes that while streptococci use extracellular polysaccharides to adhere to teeth and form plaques, this adhesion merely facilitates colonization; the true cause of caries is acid production resulting in enamel demineralization. Thus, acid production—despite not initiating attachment—is the central pathological process responsible for disease progression. 

Only include information strictly related to the clinical relevance of the query about how streptococci contribute to caries development.

The agent concludes that streptococci contribute to caries primarily through acid production during metabolic activity, which causes enamel demineralization—an essential hallmark of caries. Adherence mediated by extracellular polysaccharides supports bacterial survival but is secondary to acid-driven destruction of dentine and enamel structure.
